texto: ...in the distance across the dark fields I saw a flame. With the rainy season, the fireflies had long since disappeared. What then could this be? The flame flickered, now brightly, no dimly, and sometimes it glowed like a halo, as if it had sunk deep into water.I was frightened by this flame. For in my heart I, too, carried a flame.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shōhei Ōoka: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “...in the distance across the dark fields I saw a flame. With the rainy season, the fireflies had…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, fire, flame pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao-noturna.
